intro: Chione coriacea, also known as Patagonian wintergreen, is a flowering plant native to the southern region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering relief from inflammation and pain, as well as demonstrating antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Chione coriacea include methyl salicylate, salicylic acid, and other compounds that are known for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
